human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for capital-markets asset-servicing specificity, announcement and entitlement workflow coverage, proxy and tax-support boundaries, repeat-failure and outage escalation posture, source quality, and internal consistency across manifest, research, evaluation, deployment, and commercialization artifacts.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to asset-servicing support work such as announcements, notices, entitlements, proxy distribution, tax-relief status, returned-funds follow-up, and legal-notice handling.

Authority sources cover SEC transfer-agent operating and control guidance, DTCC workflow references for CA Web, LENS, redemptions, reorganizations, proxy services, and tax relief, plus Swift terminology normalization.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported overrides, adjacent-lane handoffs, repeat failures, conflicting notices, generic-label rejection, low-confidence escalation, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/capital-markets-and-asset-management/asset-servicing-specialist/ so servicing support stays distinct from elections, tax advice, proxy voting, claims ownership, settlements authority, and legal interpretation.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated asset-servicing specialist for capital-markets and asset-management servicing, notice, entitlement, proxy, and tax-support workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
